steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: ”As long as the evidence... is ____, it is difficult to justify the claim that the interpretation is incorrect.” This conditional structure reveals that the blank must describe a quality of evidence that makes an interpretation defensible—something that supports rather than weakens it.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: ”Reasonable” is correct because logically sound evidence makes an interpretation defensible. The other three don't work: inferior suggests weakness and easy dismissal, while identical and repetitive describe form rather than validity. That matches choice D — D is correct.
